description: LOAD builds a graph and LIST prints loaded module names in lexicographic order.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'graph.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module app {
  type = binary
  deps = lib
}
module lib {
  type = library
  deps =
}
')
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='LOAD graph.txt
LIST
', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    print(p.stdout.strip())

description: INFO prints name, type, and direct dependencies sorted lexicographically.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'graph.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module app {
  type = binary
  deps = util, lib
}
module lib {
  type = library
  deps =
}
module util {
  type = tool
  deps =
}
')
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='LOAD graph.txt
INFO app
', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    print(p.stdout.strip())

description: RDEPS prints modules that directly depend on the queried target.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'graph.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module app {
  type = binary
  deps = lib
}
module test {
  type = test
  deps = lib
}
module lib {
  type = library
  deps =
}
')
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='LOAD graph.txt
RDEPS lib
', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    print(p.stdout.strip())

description: TRANSITIVE prints recursive dependencies without duplicates.
RTRANSITIVE prints recursive reverse dependencies without duplicates.
Unresolved dependencies are tracked but do not prevent loading.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'graph.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module app {
  type = binary
  deps = lib, missing
}
module lib {
  type = library
  deps =
}
')
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='LOAD graph.txt
UNRESOLVED
', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    print(p.stdout.strip())

description: CHECK_CYCLES reports CYCLE when the graph contains a dependency cycle.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'graph.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module a {
  type = library
  deps = b
}
module b {
  type = library
  deps = a
}
')
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='LOAD graph.txt
CHECK_CYCLES
', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td, timeout=5)
    print(p.stdout.strip())

description: REMOVE deletes a module and turns remaining references to it into unresolved dependencies.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'graph.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module app {
  type = binary
  deps = lib
}
module lib {
  type = library
  deps =
}
')
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='LOAD graph.txt
REMOVE lib
LIST
UNRESOLVED
', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    print(p.stdout.strip())

description: A failed LOAD reports an error and preserves the previously loaded graph.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'good.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module app {\n  type = binary\n  deps =\n}\n')
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'bad.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module broken {\n  type = binary\n')
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='LOAD good.txt\nLOAD bad.txt\nLIST\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    lines = p.stdout.strip().splitlines()
    print(lines[0])
    print(lines[1].startswith('ERR'))
    print(lines[2])

description: Duplicate module names make LOAD fail with a deterministic error.

import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile

with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'dup.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module app {
  type = binary
  deps =
}
module app {
  type = test
  deps =
}
')
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='LOAD dup.txt
LIST
', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    lines = p.stdout.strip().splitlines()
    print(lines[0].startswith('ERR'))
    print(len(lines))

description: Invalid commands and unknown module queries emit stable error lines.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='BAD\nDEPS missing\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print(p.stdout.strip())
expected_output:
ERR invalid command
ERR unknown module

description: A second successful LOAD replaces the entire previous graph.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'g1.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module app {
  type = binary
  deps =
}
')
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'g2.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module tool {
  type = tool
  deps =
}
')
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='LOAD g1.txt
LIST
LOAD g2.txt
LIST
', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    print(p.stdout.strip())

description: INFO, DEPS, and RDEPS expose consistent views of the same directed graph.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'graph.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module app {
  type = binary
  deps = util, lib
}
module lib {
  type = library
  deps =
}
module util {
  type = tool
  deps =
}
')
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='LOAD graph.txt
DEPS app
RDEPS lib
INFO app
', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    print(p.stdout.strip())
expected_output:
OK
lib
util
app
name=app
type=binary
deps=lib,util

description: Direct edges feed both transitive and reverse-transitive traversal consistently.
expected_output:
OK
b
c
d
a
b
c

RDEPS missing
RTRANSITIVE missing

description: A failed LOAD does not poison later queries against the previous valid graph.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'good.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module app {\n  type = binary\n  deps =\n}\n')
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'bad.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module broken {\n  type = binary\n')
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='LOAD good.txt\nLOAD bad.txt\nLIST\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    lines = p.stdout.strip().splitlines()
    print(lines[0])
    print(lines[1].startswith('ERR'))
    print(lines[2])

}
module lib {
  type = library
  deps =
}
')
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'g2.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:

  type = tool
  deps =
}
')

REMOVE lib
UNRESOLVED
LOAD g2.txt
UNRESOLVED
LIST
', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)

description: LIST, DEPS, and UNRESOLVED outputs remain deterministic and lexicographic.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'graph.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module z {
  type = binary
  deps = missing2, a, missing1
}
module a {
  type = library
  deps =
}
module m {
  type = tool
  deps =
}
')
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='LOAD graph.txt
LIST
DEPS z
UNRESOLVED
', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    print(p.stdout.strip())
expected_output:
OK
a
m
z
a
missing1
missing2
missing1
missing2

with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'graph.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:

REMOVE lib
UNRESOLVED

description: A malformed dependency list with a trailing comma makes LOAD fail deterministically.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'bad.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module app {\n  type = binary\n  deps = lib,\n}\n')
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='LOAD bad.txt\nLIST\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    lines = p.stdout.strip().splitlines()
    print(lines[0].startswith('ERR'))
    print(len(lines))

description: A shared unresolved dependency participates consistently in TRANSITIVE, RDEPS, RTRANSITIVE, and UNRESOLVED queries.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'graph.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module app {
  type = binary
  deps = lib, missing
}
module lib {
  type = library
  deps = missing
}
')
    cmd = 'LOAD graph.txt
UNRESOLVED
TRANSITIVE app
RDEPS missing
RTRANSITIVE missing
'
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input=cmd, text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    print(p.stdout.strip())
expected_output:
OK
missing
lib
missing
app
lib
app
lib

description: A duplicate-module LOAD fails without replacing the previous valid graph used by later INFO queries.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'good.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module app {
  type = binary
  deps =
}
')
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'dup.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module x {
  type = library
  deps =
}
module x {
  type = test
  deps =
}
')
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='LOAD good.txt
LOAD dup.txt
INFO app
', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    lines = p.stdout.strip().splitlines()
    print(lines[0])
    print(lines[1].startswith('ERR'))
    print('
'.join(lines[2:]))

description: An empty module file is a failed LOAD and does not replace the previous valid graph with an empty graph.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'good.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module app {\n  type = binary\n  deps =\n}\n')
    open(os.path.join(td, 'empty.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8').close()
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='LOAD good.txt\nLOAD empty.txt\nLIST\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    lines = p.stdout.strip().splitlines()
    print(lines[0])
    print(lines[1].startswith('ERR'))
    print(lines[2])

description: After REMOVE mutates the graph, a malformed reload must preserve that removed-module state rather than partially rebuilding.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'good.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module app {
  type = binary
  deps = lib
}
module lib {
  type = library
  deps =
}
')
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'bad.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('module other {
  type = tool
  deps = dep,
}
')
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='LOAD good.txt
REMOVE lib
LOAD bad.txt
LIST
UNRESOLVED
', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    lines = p.stdout.strip().splitlines()
    print(lines[0])
    print(lines[1])
    print(lines[2].startswith('ERR'))
    print('
'.join(lines[3:]))
